perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information means all confidential and proprietary information which is disclosed by one party to the other party and is clearly labeled as confidential or proprietary or is disclosed orally is followed up in writing within 30 days of the oral disclosure identifying the subject matter which is confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Data means all confidential and proprietary information which is disclosed by one party to the other party and is clearly labeled as confidential or proprietary or is disclosed orally is followed up in writing within 60 days of the oral disclosure identifying the subject matter which is confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1 is changed to 'Protected Data,' and the follow-up period for oral disclosures is extended from 30 to 60 days. This creates a contradiction because the agreement refers to 'Confidential Information' in subsequent sections, implying the original 30-day follow-up period, while 'Protected Data' requires 60 days. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about which timeframe applies for oral disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above. Each party agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third person and only disclose the confidential information to its employees and those of its affiliates who have a need to know and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each party agrees that it shall protect the confidentiality of, and take reasonable steps to prevent disclosure or unauthorized use of, the Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons not legally bound to maintain its confid entiality, provided that in no event shall such party's obligations exceed the reasonable standard of care taken to protect its own confidential information of like importance. E ach party will promptly advise the other party in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information and provide assistance to the injured party in any legal proceedings related thereto. Each party acknowledges that its obligations hereunder survive in accordance with the terms hereof, notwithstanding the termination of the business relationship of the parties, for a period of two (2) years following the last disclo sure of Confidential Information by the other party hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' with a 30-day follow-up period. The changed text redefines it as 'Protected Data' with a 60-day follow-up. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information,' implying the 30-day rule. Since the changed text alters the term 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' and modifies its associated follow-up period, it directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which assumes the original 30-day timeframe for 'Confidential Information.' This creates an in-text contradiction by introducing two different follow-up periods for what is essentially the same type of information, thus adhering to the Misaligned Terminology definition and creating a strong internal inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees not to utilize the Confidential Data for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Confidential Data' in Section 2. This creates a contradiction as 'Confidential Information' is defined in Section 1, and using a different term raises doubts whether 'Confidential Data' refers to the same information with the same restrictions creating an in-text contradiction of terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No copies of any Confidential Information ma y be made except to implement the purposes of this Agreement. Any materials, documents, notes, memoranda, software code, drawings, sketches and other tangible items containing, consisting of or relating to the Confidential Information of a party which are furnished hereunder to the other party, or are in the possession of the other party, remain the property of the party which disclosed the Confidential Information and shall be promptly returned to such party upon the disclosing party’s request therefore. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, by license or otherwise, in any Confidential Information except as specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data'. The 'Contradicted Text' section repeatedly uses 'Confidential Information', which is a defined term in Section 1 (as indicated in the 'Perturbation Explanation'). By using 'Confidential Data' instead, it creates uncertainty whether the 'Contradicted Text' still applies to 'Confidential Data', thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Any materials, documents, notes, memoranda, software code, drawings, sketches and other tangible items containing, consisting of or relating to the Confidential Information of a party which are furnished hereunder to the other party, or are in the possession of the other party, remain the property of the party which disclosed the Confidential Information and shall be promptly returned to such party upon the disclosing party’s request therefore.
perturbation.changed_text: Any documents, notes, memoranda, software code, drawings, sketches and other tangible items containing, consisting of or relating to the Protected Information of a party which are furnished hereunder to the other party, or are in the possession of the other party, remain the property of the party which disclosed the Protected Information and shall be promptly returned to such party upon the disclosing party’s request therefore.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Protected Information' in Section 3. This change introduces an in-text contradiction, as Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information' and this section refers to 'Protected Information' without a definition, leading to uncertainty about whether these terms are interchangeable or have different scopes. This impacts the return obligations regarding the specified items.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement shall not apply to information which: (a) is known by the receiving party or is publicly available at the time of disclosure; (b) becomes publicly available after disclosure by the disclosing party to the receiving party through no act of either party; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to the receiving party by a third party without restriction as to use or disclosure; (d) is disclosed with the prior written consent of the disclosing party; (e) is information that was independently developed by the receiving party; or (f) is required to be disclosed pursuant to any judicial or administrative proceeding, provided that the receiving party promptly notifies the disclosing party of such action and gives the disclosing party the opportunity to seek any legal remedies to maintain such information in confidence.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' introduces a contradiction because Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information,' and the altered Section 3 then uses 'Protected Information' without a definition. This misalignment of terminology creates uncertainty as to whether the obligations regarding the return of documents apply to 'Protected Information', contradicting the intent of the original text regarding 'Confidential Information'. Since the Contradicted Text specifies what information is excluded from obligations, the change in terminology introduces ambiguity, directly impacting the understanding and enforcement of the agreement. Therefore, the misaligned terminology leads to an in-text contradiction as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement shall not apply to information which: (a) is known by the receiving party or is publicly available at the time of disclosure; (b) becomes publicly available after disclosure by the disclosing party to the receiving party through no act of either party; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to the receiving party by a third party without restriction as to use or disclosure; (d) is disclosed with the prior written consent of the disclosing party; (e) is information that was independently developed by the receiving party; or (f) is required to be disclosed pursuant to any judicial or administrative proceeding, provided that the receiving party promptly notifies the disclosing party of such action and gives the disclosing party the opportunity to seek any legal remedies to maintain such information in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement shall not apply to data which: (a) is known by the receiving party or is publicly available at the time of disclosure; (b) becomes publicly available after disclosure by the disclosing party to the receiving party through no act of either party; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to the receiving party by a third party without restriction as to use or disclosure; (d) is disclosed with the prior written consent of the disclosing party; (e) is data that was independently developed by the receiving party; or (f) is required to be disclosed pursuant to any judicial or administrative proceeding, provided that the receiving party promptly notifies the disclosing party of such action and gives the disclosing party the opportunity to seek any legal remedies to maintain such data in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'information' is replaced with 'data' in Section 4. This creates an in-text contradiction as the contract defines 'Confidential Information' (or its altered versions) but does not define 'data.' It is unclear whether 'data' encompasses all forms of 'Confidential Information' or has a different scope and whether the exceptions listed apply equally to 'data'. Also, the change to 'data that was independently developed' potentially changes the meaning and scope of the exception, creating contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information means all confidential and proprietary information which is disclosed by one party to the other party and is clearly labeled as confidential or proprietary or is disclosed orally is followed up in writing within 30 days of the oral disclosure identifying the subject matter which is confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' in the changed text, coupled with the fact that the contract defines 'Confidential Information' but not 'data', creates an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information', implying a specific scope. By replacing 'information' with 'data' without defining 'data', it's unclear whether the scope of what is protected remains the same. This uncertainty constitutes a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall obligate either party to disclose any information to the other party or enter into any other agreement with th e other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Agreement shall obligate either party to disclose any data to the other party or enter into any other agreement with th e other party.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'information' is replaced by 'data' in Section 5. This is an in-text contradiction. While Section 4 also uses the word 'data', this section previously referred to 'information' which, while used generally, is assumed by a reader to refer back to 'Confidential Information' as defined earlier. By now using 'data,' this term now seemingly refers to everything that is being disclosed, and is therefore now broader than 'Confidential Information.' If something is not 'Confidential Information', is it protected under this NDA? This change impacts the disclosure obligations, as it is unclear whether 'data' and 'Confidential Information' are equivalent terms in the context of obligatory disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purposes set forth above. Each party agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information to any third person and only disclose the confidential information to its employees and those of its affiliates who have a need to know and who agree to keep such information confidential. Each party agrees that it shall protect the confidentiality of, and take reasonable steps to prevent disclosure or unauthorized use of, the Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons not legally bound to maintain its confid entiality, provided that in no event shall such party's obligations exceed the reasonable standard of care taken to protect its own confidential information of like importance. E ach party will promptly advise the other party in writing of any misappropriation or misuse by any person of such Confidential Information and provide assistance to the injured party in any legal proceedings related thereto. Each party acknowledges that its obligations hereunder survive in accordance with the terms hereof, notwithstanding the termination of the business relationship of the parties, for a period of two (2) years following the last disclo sure of Confidential Information by the other party hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'information' which is understood to refer back to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces 'information' with 'data'. This broadens the scope of what is not obligated to be disclosed, making it unclear whether 'data', which now seemingly refers to everything disclosed, and 'Confidential Information' are equivalent terms. This directly impacts the obligations in the contradicted section which states, 'Each party agrees not to use the Confidential Information...' If 'data' is not 'Confidential Information', it's not protected under the NDA, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that if the other party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this Agreement, the damages will be irreparable and difficult to meas ure and that the other party shall be entitled to equitable relief including a preliminary injuncti on in addition to any other relief available.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that if the other party is required to bring an action to enforce the provisions of this document, the damages will be irreparable and difficult to measure and that the other party shall be entitled to equitable relief including a preliminary injunction in addition to any other relief available.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Agreement' is replaced with 'document' in Section 5 (continuing the same paragraph). This is an in-text contradiction. It's unclear if all provisions in the overall document should be considered equally enforceable, especially since earlier clauses use different terms (Confidential Information, Protected Data, data). This impacts the enforceability of specific obligations and remedies for breaching confidentiality, as it casts doubt on what the intended meaning is given changes from 'agreement' to 'document'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information means all confidential and proprietary information which is disclosed by one party to the other party and is clearly labeled as confidential or proprietary or is disclosed orally is followed up in writing within 30 days of the oral disclosure identifying the subject matter which is confidential or proprietary.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to an 'Agreement' and specifies that damages for breaching its provisions would be irreparable. The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'document'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information', which would be governed differently if document is used instead of Agreement. The explanation notes that this introduces ambiguity about which obligations are enforceable and impacts remedies for breaching confidentiality. By using 'document' instead of 'Agreement,' the change creates uncertainty about whether the confidentiality provisions are equally enforceable.
